feed.title: Mozilla
feed.subtitle: Combined Mozilla feed: the Mozilla, Firefox Nightly, Add-ons, Hacks and Thunderbird blogs, Planet Mozilla, Nightly release notes, shipped Firefox desktop and Android release notes, and Mozilla security advisories (MFSA).

entry.title: New installs of Firefox automatically enabling "Open Firefox automatically when your computer starts up" -- NO NO NO!
entry.content: Who in the world decided that it was a good idea for "Open Firefox automatically when your computer starts up" to AUTOMATICALLY be checked for everyone? This is BAD. It is ANNOYING. I'm not sure exactly what version this happened (somewhere around 152?). PLEASE STOP THIS. I am talking about setting up a new system (Windows), with a new/fresh install of Firefox, and now everyone that logs into the computer, whenever they start Firefox for the first time it AUTOMATICALLY chooses the option "Op

entry.title: Updates all the time
entry.content: I never seen a site have as many updates as firefox. It is starting to get annoying.

entry.title: Enter key doesn't send messages in the AI Chatbot sidebar — please add this
entry.content: I want to add my voice to this request. The AI Chatbot sidebar is genuinely useful for quick questions while browsing, but not being able to press Enter to send a message makes it frustrating to use every single time. Every other chat tool I use — Slack, Discord, even claude.ai and ChatGPT in a regular tab — lets me press Enter to send and Shift+Enter for a new line. It's such a basic, expected behavior that having to reach for the mouse and click the send button every time genuinely breaks my t

entry.title: Address bar focus outline appears visually misaligned in Firefox Nova UI
entry.content: I’m testing the new Firefox Nova appearance and noticed a visual issue with the address bar focus state. When the address bar is selected, the purple focus outline around the bar appears visually awkward on the left side, especially around the search engine icon/dropdown area. The inner rounded section and the outer focus border seem to overlap or align inconsistently, making the address bar look slightly unfinished. This does not appear to affect functionality, but it impacts the visual polish

entry.title: Enable the css transform property for pseudo elements
entry.content: Please in Firefox CSS enable the 'transform' property for pseudo elements (::-moz-tree-image), in my case 'rotate' on hover and select in the 'sidebar'.

entry.title: Firefox for Android 152.0.5
entry.content: Firefox for Android 152.0.5 release notes.

entry.title: Firefox 152.0.5
entry.content: Firefox 152.0.5 release notes.

entry.title: Accessing my webmail email server now blocked by Firefox
entry.content: I was able previously to access my webmail email server ( https://webmail.centurylink.net/ ) but Firefox is now completely blocking my access. The details I get back from Firefox is: Secure Connection Failed The page you are trying to view cannot be shown because the authenticity of the received data could not be verified. What can you do about it? The issue is most likely with the website, and there is nothing you can do to resolve it. You can notify the website’s administrator about the probl

entry.title: Audio not working on firefox after the last update.
entry.content: After the new update to from Version 152.0.3 (64-bit) to Version 152.0.4 (64-bit), the audio on Firefox stop working when I reopen Firefox(its only occur when I open,close and open again the browser). If anyone has an idea on what is going on or how to solve it, I will really appreciate.

entry.title: Giving Pontoon’s Editor Its Own Theme
entry.content: Each year, Mozilla welcomes interns who work alongside our engineering teams on projects that ship to production and improve the experience for contributors around the world. This year, Ayush joined … Read more

entry.title: Uber Doordash and Lyft Account Creation and Reactivation
entry.content: Do you need an account for Uber, DoorDash, or Lyft? Reach out to us today for more details on how we can help you set up a new account in no time or reactivate an existing one. HOW WE OPERATE 1) If you want a new account, you'll need to answer some questions first. This helps us understand the type of account and conditions you require. 2) If you're looking to reactivate an account, we'll start by figuring out why it was deactivated before finding a solution. 3) After we create or reactivate you

entry.title: Firefox now supports new Iterator.prototype.chunks and Iterator.prototype.windows methods that...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2047997 Firefox now supports new Iterator.prototype.chunks and Iterator.prototype.windows methods that allow consuming an iterator as either overlapping or non-overlapping subsequences of configurable size.

entry.title: The address bar now includes a "Manage AI" quick action that opens the AI section of Settings.
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 2038834 The address bar now includes a "Manage AI" quick action that opens the AI section of Settings.

entry.title: In Firefox for Android, improved the reliability of automatic translations for users with...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 1975878 In Firefox for Android , improved the reliability of automatic translations for users with multiple languages selected in system settings.

entry.title: A new Firefox look, hidden features, and more
entry.content: Hi Mozillians, welcome to another Mozilla community roundup! This month, we’re taking a look at what’s next for Firefox. From an upcoming visual refresh and a peek behind the new … Read more

entry.title: Please stop making unproductive changes to the UI
entry.content: Change simply for the sake of change is counter-productive. The recent change to the UI for "Settings" only serves to add confusion for long time users. I am speaking particularly about the change to the stored password section of the settings but it would also apply to any change when there is little or no benefit to the change. I can see absolutely no benefit to the way the UI was changed for reviewing and accessing stored passwords and the change only makes finding the functionality more d

entry.title: Workspace, Persintent pinned tabs, Folders (Zen Browser) and Web panels (Vivaldi).
entry.content: 1. Workspace (Zen) - The ability to separate tabs by context (e.g., "Work") within a single window. This eliminates tab clutter without forcing users to open multiple browser windows. Classification: Firefox's current containers are inconvenient and less intuitive than Zen's workspace. 2. Persistent pinned tabs and Folders (Zen) - Full folder support for organizing active tabs in the sidebar, along with "global" pinned tabs/folders that stay accessible across all created workspace. 3. Web panels

entry.title: Add a Built-in AI-Powered Tab Organization Feature in Firefox
entry.content: Many Firefox users regularly work with dozens of open tabs for research, work, study, and personal browsing. Managing large numbers of tabs can quickly become overwhelming. I would love to see Firefox introduce an optional AI-powered tab organization feature that can: Automatically group related tabs by topic. Suggest meaningful names for tab groups. Detect duplicate tabs and recommend closing them. Highlight inactive tabs that haven't been used for a long time. Allow users to save and restore o

entry.title: What’s the Best Streaming Service Right Now?
entry.content: With so many streaming services available today, finding a reliable one can feel overwhelming. I’m on the hunt for a platform that offers stable playback, crisp picture quality, live sports, international channels, and a solid on-demand library—all while working seamlessly across multiple devices. During my research, I came across this helpful comparison guide that breaks down popular services and what they offer. I also stumbled upon OTTOcean , which seems to get positive reviews for its channe

entry.title: How I Built a Simple Website for a Local Beach Bar in Barbados — Any Tips to Improve?
entry.content: Hi everyone! 👋 I recently helped a small local business in Barbados build their online presence and wanted to share my experience — plus get some feedback from this community. 🏝 ️ THE BUSINESS: Barefoot In The Bar — a beachfront bar and restaurant located on Queens Street, Speightstown, Saint Peter, Barbados. They serve authentic Bajan food, handcrafted rum cocktails, cold beers, and host live music every weekend right on the sand. 🌐 THE WEBSITE: I created a simple, clean website for them using

entry.title: Improve Mail editor
entry.content: The current mail editor frequently creates bad html, does not automatically insert numbers or bullets (like most editors) and has trouble with nested lists. It also frequently is literally unable to insert text. Suggest either replacing it yourself, or allowing access to other editors.

entry.title: ud not have to explain to you every time I use your browser that it's not a new device it's always been the same one .
entry.content: Iwhen I use your product I shouldn't have to tell you every time it's not a new device. I realize my situation is probably unique since I'm not using your normal logic gated system and mine is autonomous as well as kinetic but that shouldn't stop you from seeing I have never changed devices or location. And I would consistently use your services if when I did you didn't fail to execute what I want in my searches and allowing me to connect but you rely too much on Google services and they are let

entry.title: Best Replica bag website?
entry.content: I've been researching different replica bag websites because there are so many options available today, and it's becoming difficult to know which sellers actually offer good quality. I'm mainly looking for premium craftsmanship, accurate details, quality materials, QC photos before shipping, and reliable customer service. While comparing different stores, I came across LuxuryRepBags , which seems to receive positive feedback for its 1:1 replica designer bags, attention to detail, and wide select

entry.title: Windows VPN Server: A Smart Way to Secure Your Connection
entry.content: A Windows VPN server is a practical solution for anyone who wants to improve online privacy and securely access files or devices from different locations. Whether you work remotely, travel frequently, or simply value your digital security, a VPN server can help protect your internet traffic from unauthorized access. Windows includes built-in VPN server capabilities, making it possible to create secure connections without requiring expensive hardware. Once configured, users can remotely connect t

entry.title: HomwRun IPTV: A Convenient Way to Stream Live TV
entry.content: HomwRun IPTV is becoming a popular search term among people looking for a flexible way to watch live television online. IPTV (Internet Protocol Television) uses an internet connection to deliver live TV channels, movies, sports, news, and on-demand content, offering an alternative to traditional cable and satellite services. One of the biggest advantages of HomwRun IPTV is the ability to stream content across multiple devices, including Smart TVs, Android phones, iPhones, tablets, Fire TV Stick,

entry.title: IPTVTune – A Feature-Rich IPTV Streaming Service
entry.content: IPTVTune is an IPTV streaming service that provides access to live TV channels, movies, TV series, sports, and on-demand entertainment over an internet connection. It is designed for users who want an alternative to traditional cable or satellite television by offering content across multiple devices, including Smart TVs, Android devices, Fire TV Stick, smartphones, tablets, and PCs. Reported features include HD and 4K streaming support, Electronic Program Guide (EPG), Video on Demand (VOD), and

entry.title: Best IPTV Services: How to Choose a Safe and Reliable Option
entry.content: Finding the best IPTV service requires more than comparing prices. A quality IPTV provider should offer reliable streaming, high-definition video, a user-friendly interface, and access to legally licensed live TV channels and on-demand content. With so many options available, choosing a trusted service helps ensure a better viewing experience while protecting your privacy and security. Well-known streaming platforms such as YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, Sling TV, Fubo, and DIRECTV Stream are popul

entry.title: Live Sports Streaming Without Cable: The Best Way to Watch Your Favorite Games
entry.content: Watching your favorite teams has never been easier thanks to live sports streaming without cable . As more fans move away from traditional cable subscriptions, streaming services now provide access to major sporting events with greater flexibility and lower monthly costs. Whether you enjoy football, basketball, baseball, soccer, tennis, or racing, there are legal streaming platforms that deliver live coverage in high definition. Popular services such as YouTube TV, Fubo, Hulu + Live TV, Sling TV

entry.title: Cheapest Streaming TV Services: Affordable Ways to Watch Live TV
entry.content: Finding the cheapest streaming TV services is easier than ever, thanks to the growing number of affordable platforms that offer live TV and on-demand entertainment. Whether you're replacing cable or looking to reduce your monthly expenses, budget-friendly streaming services provide excellent value without sacrificing quality. Popular options include Sling TV, Philo, Frndly TV, and other low-cost streaming providers. These services offer a variety of channels covering news, entertainment, lifesty

entry.title: Cable TV Alternatives 2026: The Best Ways to Stream Live Entertainment
entry.content: As streaming technology continues to improve, cable TV alternatives 2026 are becoming the preferred choice for millions of viewers. Modern streaming services offer live TV, on-demand content, sports, news, and entertainment without expensive contracts or hidden equipment fees. Whether you're looking to save money or enjoy greater flexibility, there are plenty of options available. Leading services such as YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, Sling TV, Fubo, and DIRECTV Stream provide extensive channel li

entry.title: Watch Live TV Online Legally: A Safe and Smart Way to Stream
entry.content: If you want to watch live TV online legally , there are more options than ever before. Licensed streaming services let you enjoy live sports, breaking news, movies, and popular TV channels without relying on unauthorized websites. Choosing a legal platform ensures better video quality, reliable performance, and a safer browsing experience. Some of the most popular services include YouTube TV, Hulu + Live TV, Sling TV, Fubo, and DIRECTV Stream. Each offers different channel packages and pricing,

entry.title: Best Live TV Streaming Service: Which One Is Right for You?
entry.content: Choosing the best live TV streaming service depends on your viewing habits, budget, and favorite channels. With cable prices continuing to rise, streaming platforms offer a flexible and affordable way to watch live sports, news, entertainment, and local channels without long-term contracts. Popular services like YouTube TV , Hulu + Live TV , Sling TV , Fubo , and DIRECTV Stream each provide unique advantages. YouTube TV is known for its unlimited DVR and broad channel lineup, while Hulu + Live T

entry.title: How often should I turn a bedbound patient to avoid bedsores?
entry.content: There are many ways to avoid bedsores a bedridden pation, with the help of Careoxy attendant at home to chnage position timely. Yes, the 2-hour rule is the gold standard, but you can customize it slightly with a schedule like 'Back, Right Side, Back, Left Side.' Use pillows to prop up her back and a pillow between her knees to stop her knees and ankles from rubbing together. Most importantly, float the heels —place a pillow under her calves so her heels hover off the mattress. Check her tailbone

entry.title: YouTube Monetization
entry.content: Ever wondered what it takes to operate a successful YouTube channel? It is not about posting videos and wishing that everything will go alright. YouTube monetization services provide the resources and experience that would help transform your passion into a profitable business. But what are the criteria to be monetized in 2025? Let’s break it down. Your channel must satisfy some requirements in order to be eligible for Youtube monetizer services : 1,000 Subscribers : Your channel should have a m

entry.title: Communications frustrations
entry.content: I'm a long-term very satisfied Firefox user - 4.5 min/5. I recognise the great complexity of your systems. But I've just wasted a hugely frustrating hour or more searching for your blithely stated 'find your group tabs at any time under list all tabs'. Splendid - simplicity. Finally Google told me said cryptic list hides under an inconspicuous down arrow - simple, indeed. Forgive me if I'm wrong, but I can't find anywhere that Firefox provides this info. It's difficult to cover all bases,

entry.title: Picture-in-Picture mode
entry.content: Hi, can you do something to avoid full screen or narrow it when I press space bar? It is really annoying since I use a lossless scaling with PIP to improve the video but when I need to pause by pressing spacebar, it just goes out of full screen. Would be great if you also work in lossless scaling technology integrated in Firefox, none other browser has it and could attract people that spend a lot of time in the browser.

entry.title: stop stopping and aking me if i want to stop
entry.content: instead of asking me "this page is slowing down firefox do you want to stop it" can you just do what i want you to do? i am using chat gpt and you stop about half a dozen times while the chat bot is running. how about instead of stopping you just do what i want you to do and run.

entry.title: GrapheneOS Advises Against Firefox on Android. Can Mozilla Address These Claims Publicly?
entry.content: outdated criticisms of Firefox's sandboxing and security has become popular, mostly through competitor browser affiliated people GrapheneOS, the hardening-focused Android OS, advises users against installing Firefox. Their official usage guide makes several specific security claims about GeckoView [1]: 1. "Firefox does not have internal sandboxing on Android" 2. Gecko-based browsers are "much more vulnerable to exploitation" 3. Firefox "bypass or cripple a fair bit of the upstream and GrapheneOS

entry.title: Firefox The Source of ALL my Browsing Problems
entry.content: I have been battling with browsing In Firefox for well....ever since you guys changed everything, a couple years back. Constantly running out of memory with only 3 or 4 tabs open, to the point of crashing my whole computer and having to do a reboot, the loss of Data, plus the pure frustration of losing patience with how slow everything was. It was not unusual for pages to take many minutes to load, and often involved numerous lockups and reloads. Now I admit..my poor old Laptop Is not top of the

entry.title: Border corners
entry.content: Hello. I encountered one nasty bug in Firefox: When I create a table with two columns, where the first column has borders and two cells, while the second column has no borders and is set to rowspan=2, the result is ugly. See how it looks: http://primerecords.dk/Wikipedia/border-collapse.html Both corners in the second column are broken. I consulted my friends at Wikipedia about this problem. They confirmed that this problem is only in Firefox; in other browsers, the border corners are displayed

entry.title: Mozilla Mornings comes to the UK: privacy-enhancing technologies and the questions they raise
entry.content: During London Tech Week, Mozilla hosted the first UK edition of Mozilla Mornings, our breakfast-discussion series on the digital questions of the moment. We brought together technologists, policymakers, industry, civil … Read more The post Mozilla Mornings comes to the UK: privacy-enhancing technologies and the questions they raise appeared first on Open Policy & Advocacy.

entry.title: Best 13 Healthcare App Development Companies in Montana (2026)
entry.content: Montana is not the place that comes to mind when people think of a place with a lot of healthcare technology.. Montana has a lot of rural areas where it is hard to get good healthcare and a growing number of technology companies in Bozeman and Billings. This has created a need for health tools, such as telehealth platforms that can work well even with slow internet connections, remote patient monitoring systems for people who live far apart and scheduling and electronic health record systems tha

entry.title: What is the Best Way to Back Up Microsoft 365 Emails for Long-Term Storage?
entry.content: Hi everyone, I need to back up several Microsoft 365 mailboxes for long-term storage and as a safeguard against accidental deletion or data loss. While Microsoft 365 offers retention policies, I'm looking for a solution that creates an independent backup that I can restore whenever needed. My requirements are: Backup multiple Microsoft 365 accounts Export emails to formats like PST, MBOX, EML, or PDF Preserve the original folder structure and email metadata Support incremental backups to avoid d

entry.title: I Request a Non-AI Based Translation Feature
entry.content: I would very much like to use the browser's translaton feature but I do not understand why the browser's translation option has to depend on the "AI" features built into the browser given that translation methods have been available for a long time before LLMs and such existed. I will not enable the AI features just for the convenience of a translation tool, but I don't see why I should in the first place given AI isn't necessary. I have been experimenting with AI from many different companies a

entry.title: A opção "(Perguntar onde salvar arquivos antes de baixar)" NÃO ESTÁ FUNCIONANDO CORRETAMENTE!
entry.content: Boa noite! A opção "(Perguntar onde salvar arquivos antes de baixar)" NÃO ESTÁ FUNCIONANDO CORRETAMENTE! Sempre usei este navegador, é meu navegador padrão! e sempre uso a opção Perguntar onde salvar arquivos antes de baixar ativada para que eu escolho o local onde cada arquivo deve ser baixado! e de uns dias para cá essa função mesmo ativada ela nao esta funcionando! ja desativei ela e reiniciei o navegador, e ativei novamente mas ela nao esta funcionando!

entry.title: Firefox's Local Network Access protections now extends to WebSocket connections. Websites that...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 2042339 Firefox's Local Network Access protections now extends to WebSocket connections. Websites that try to open a WebSocket to a device on your local network will now ask for your permission first

entry.title: Added support for the No-Vary-Search HTTP response header for the HTTP disk cache. Servers can...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2038013 Added support for the No-Vary-Search HTTP response header for the HTTP disk cache. Servers can now declare which URL query parameters are irrelevant to the response, allowing Firefox to reuse cached resources across parameter variations and improving cache hit rates.

entry.title: Mozilla Update Release - Inconvenient
entry.content: When in the middle of the day when I have 20 browser windows open and they all freeze so that Firefox can download a new release, that discombobulates the items I was working on. I should be notified and allowed to defer the update until I am ready to shut everything down. Please follow common change control rules. You want a reputation of being courteous. When you do not, it can affect profit and loss for day traders, and many other professions. Thank you for your attention and consideration.

entry.title: Update! Update! Update! And Update! Endless updates!
entry.content: When I open Firefox for an urgent task, it's an update! If I open it five times a day, it's an update five times! Update, update! Endless updates! Updates tirelessly, until I'm fed up! I'm about to do something, and Firefox keeps updating as if it's assassinating my attention! Without asking me, the stupid Firefox update page opens! As if it's essential! I'm so curious and it's vital for me! I'm ending my Firefox adventure solely because of this update, update, update madness. You can shove your

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the CSS Typed Object Model API (Typed...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2051047 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the CSS Typed Object Model API (Typed OM) . This API allows web applications and other Web Platform features to work with CSS values through typed JavaScript objects instead of strings.

entry.title: I have been locked out of Afterpay by firefox update since 30-6-26
entry.content: Has driven me up the wall was it afterpay upgrade, firefox upgrade, ebay could eBay have done it no none of the above. It was AI yes stupid dumb no brains ai. Apparently Microsoft have created an AI thing that can automaticly write it's own fix for defender and update our computers that we paid for and without consent or notification and turn out computers into boat anchors. checkout the update numbers with google. The fix is to go into defender turn off encription settings, app and browser con

entry.title: useful keyboard shortcuts for navigating tabs list and vertical tabs
entry.content: # Feature Requests ## Tabs List ### Allow Tabs List to be opened with a keyboard shortcut in linear Tab cycle mode Currently, the "Tabs List" can be opened using a configurable keyboard shortcut (the default is `Shift+Ctrl+Tab`), but only when the "Ctrl+Tab cycles through tabs in recently used order" setting is enabled. ### Provide configurable keyboard shortcuts to scroll the Tabs List Currently the Tabs List - once focused - can be scrolled by - pressing `Tab` or `Shift+Tab` repeatedly - using

entry.title: Browser fingerprinting compromise
entry.content: Please see the attached screenshot. Any information or feedback will be much appreciated.

entry.title: Mozilla Connect Monthly Recap: Top-Voted Ideas for June 2026
entry.content: Hey all, Happy July and welcome to another edition of our Monthly Recap —not to be confused with our Weekly Recap (which we publish every Friday). This post will highlight the top 15 most-voted ideas for the previous month. While some are ‘ new ideas’ and others may already be ‘in development’ ( learn more about The Idea Journey here ), we encourage you to continue voting and sharing your feedback—the more details, the better. Our teams are regularly looking at ideas and threads here as they pla

entry.title: Netflix forces logout/crash when trying to play any video content
entry.content: Hello, I am experiencing an issue with Netflix on Zen Browser (Desktop). I can log in to my account without any issues and browse the catalog. However, the moment I click "Play" on any movie or TV show, an error occurs ("Si è verificato un problema" / An error occurred) and Zen immediately logs me out of my Netflix account. This seems to be related to DRM (Widevine) handshaking or Advanced Tracking Protection/Fingerprinting resistance blocking the video stream session tokens. OS: Windows 11

entry.title: Best SMTP server for email marketing
entry.content: Can anyone recommend the best SMTP server for email marketing? I recently came across SMTPmart while comparing different providers, but I am not sure how it performs compared to SendGrid or Amazon SES. Would love to hear some real user experiences before making a decision.

entry.title: Problems wish 152.0.4
entry.content: Version 152.0.4 freezes and is unusable...

entry.title: Pretty unusal
entry.content: Wuuu That seems pretty unusual 🤣 (Appeared after opening FF again when sudden PC power off by electricity gone.) Windows 11

entry.title: This Week in Rust 658
New-look Firefox
entry.content: I'm a long-time Firefox user (well over 20 years) and this is the first time, I think, that I am writing to you. And my purpose in writing is simply to convey my appreciation for your excellent browser, now made even better with the most recent changes. I'm 76 years old and keep all of my software - from Windows 11 Pro and all my applications, including Firefox and Thunderbird, up-to-date 24/7. Thanks again for all that you do.

entry.title: Happy BMO Push Day! (20260630.1)
entry.content:
Github Link The following changes have been pushed to bugzilla.mozilla.org:
Bug 1726931 - New issues should displayed on “My Dashboard” for first time users instead of assigned bugs Bug 1834454 - [WEBHOOKS] Webhooks for a product change don’t fire if product is changed away from the target product Bug 1684509 - Have a single button to mark a bug as abusive/spam
Discuss these changes in the BMO Matrix Room 1 post - 1 participant Read full topic

entry.title: Why am I connecting to Mozilla Forums with Chromium browser ?
entry.content: I am running Ubuntu 24.04 with XFCE desktop, a relatively lightweight environment, and 8GB RAM. I currently am connected to this forum using the chromium browser, because Firefox periodically brings my system to a nearly complete stop. Firefox is my preferred browser, but it is truly becoming unusable. I have neither the skill set, the inclination, or the time to try to figure out why this is happening. At various times, Firefox uses essentially all CPU resources, to the point that is takes many

entry.title: Firestorm funtioniert nicht mehr.
entry.content: Hallo. Seit dem letzten Update funktoniert die Website von Amazon nicht mehr.. und mein eMail Login auch nicht... ich benutze jetzt Microsoft Edge mit Windows 10 damit funktioniert alles! Was habt ihr gemacht bei dem letzten Update... viele Passwörter werden nicht mehr erkannt... und fals die Frage kommt .. Windows 10 hätte kein Support mehr... ich habe noch support bis 2032!

entry.title: bug firefox imac sonoma 14.8.7
entry.content: salve, siete capaci per favore di fixare su imac con sonoma in oggetto firefox in modo tale che smetta di comparire la rotellina colorata ogni qual volta refresho la pagina ??? grazie

entry.title: Best IPTV Service 2026: Why IPTVGreat is the #1 Choice Worldwide
entry.content: Finding the Best IPTV Service can be overwhelming with so many options available. After extensive research and real user feedback, one provider consistently rises above the competition: IPTVGreat . Why IPTVGreat is the Best IPTV Provider IPTVGreat has earned its reputation as the Best IPTV provider through seven years of trusted service. With a stellar 4.93/5 rating on RatingFacts from verified customers, it delivers what others only promise. Unmatched Content Library As the Best IPTV 2026 , IPT

entry.title: Similar UIs
entry.content: While I understand that there are limitations in screen space, processing, and functionality in mobile apps, I do not understand why the android app (never experienced the apple version) is NOTHING like the PC counterpart. i mean, it does not function the same on android, the UI is completely lacking and foreign, and the robust nature of the wonderful PC version is almost completely lost on android. Using minimization techniques/buttons and other space saving features such as pop-up and drop

entry.title: MFSA 2026-63 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 152.0.1
entry.content: MFSA 2026-63 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 152.0.1

entry.content: MFSA 2026-62 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Firefox 152.0.4

entry.title: Firefox for Android 152.0.4
entry.content: Firefox for Android 152.0.4 release notes.

entry.title: Firefox 152.0.4
entry.content: Firefox 152.0.4 release notes.

entry.title: Announcing Rust 1.96.1
entry.content:
The Rust team has published a new point release of Rust, 1.96.1. Rust is a programming language that is empowering everyone to build reliable and efficient software. If you have a previous version of Rust installed via rustup, getting Rust 1.96.1 is as easy as: rustup update stable If you don't have it already, you can get rustup from the appropriate page on our website.
What's in 1.96.1 Rust 1.96.1 fixes:
Missing retries / timeouts in Cargo's HTTP client Miscompilation in a MIR optimization

entry.title: May in Servo: user scripts, mp4 compat, blackboxing in DevTools, and more!
entry.content:
Servo 0.3.0 contains all of the changes we landed in May, which came out to 391 commits (March: 534). For security fixes, see § Security.
We’ve shipped several new web platform features:
‘font-kerning: none’ (@simonwuelker, #44634) ‘font-variant-east-asian’ (@simonwuelker, #44989) ‘font-variant-ligatures’ (@simonwuelker, #44903) ‘font-variant-numeric’ (@simonwuelker, #44950) ‘font-variant-position’ (@simonwuelker, #45142) mp4 files without fast start in (@calvaris, #45084) (@yezhizhen, #4502

entry.title: Will Mozilla stop using Mullvad for their VPN backend following the news that one of their founders is a major backer for a far right political activist group?
entry.content: It deeply concerns me to consider that I am trusting an organization who would partner with people like this with all of my, at times, sensitive browsing data. I recently read about the update where Mozilla VPN was integrated into Firefox and I want to be certain that none of my information is being sent to them. I also don't want to financially support Mullvad, even inadvertently, by supporting Mozilla if there is no plan put in place to end this partnership within a reasonable time frame.

entry.title: “Add to Apple Wallet” button compatibility
entry.content: I can never use “add to Apple Wallet” buttons in Firefox. I have to switch over to Safari. Would love for this to not break in Firefox all the time!

entry.title: Location Request
entry.content: Recent chat with Duck.ai.... I I suggested that asking to use location when general location was .ai's need would be more appropriate. It suggested forwarding the thought to programmers via this feedback. =======\Duck.ai response======== You’re right—when location is needed, asking for **general location** (e.g., ZIP code/city/region) is usually sufficient and safer than precise location. I can’t directly forward requests to programmers, but if you want, you can paste your exact wording into the

entry.title: Firefox now supports the Iterator.prototype.join method that allows joining an iterator into a...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2047995 Firefox now supports the Iterator.prototype.join method that allows joining an iterator into a string, similar to Array.prototype.join.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2050138 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the text-box-trim and text-box-edge CSS properties, and the associated text-box shorthand. These properties allow you to control the leading space above and below text, enabling simpler and consistent vertical spacing and alignment.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports QUIC version 2 (RFC 9369) for HTTP/3...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 2040450 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports QUIC version 2 ( RFC 9369 ) for HTTP/3 connections. Firefox offers both QUIC v1 and v2 using compatible version negotiation ( RFC 9368 ). The server selects which version is used, so connections upgrade to QUIC v2 when the server chooses it and remain on QUIC v1 otherwise.

entry.title: VPN disabled with proxy
entry.content: When automatic proxy configuration is enabled VPN does not work. It turns green, but the IP address remains my own. It does not work with normal nor InPrivate modes. To turn it on, one must disable proxy and restart FF

entry.title: 'X' mark visible on mouse over a tab to close tab
entry.content: Currently it is tedious to close a set of tabs. One has to use multiple steps or click on the tab and then the 'X' mark appears to close the tab. I think it will be a lot more productive if 'X' mark is visible on hovering the cursor over a particular tab with no more clicks involved. Currently only the extreme right tab shows an active 'X' mark

entry.title: Improving Transparency and Assurance in the Web PKI: Mozilla Root Store Policy v3.1
entry.content: Mozilla remains committed to maintaining a secure, trustworthy, and transparent Web PKI. Today we are announcing the publication of Mozilla Root Store Policy (MRSP) version 3.1, effective July 1, 2026. … Read more The post Improving Transparency and Assurance in the Web PKI: Mozilla Root Store Policy v3.1 appeared first on Mozilla Security Blog.

entry.title: Firefox Themed Icon Logo Too Large
entry.content: At least for me, on Android 17 with a Google Pixel 9 Pro XL, the Firefox logo in the app's themed icon is too big in the icon's circle, making it get too close to the edges. It's a very small detail, yes, but the logo is noticeably larger in the themed icon than in the normal app icon, and having such little padding between the edges of the logo and the edges of the app icon makes it stick out noticeably against all the other themed icons, which goes against the point of having themed icons in t

entry.title: It crashes due to ATOK.
entry.content: Firefox crashes when using the "ATOK" Kana-Kanji conversion application. The OS slows down, forcing a restart.

entry.title: Firefox Profiler Deployment (June 29, 2026)
entry.content:
The latest version of the Firefox Profiler is now live! Check out the full changelog below to see what’s changed: Highlights:
[Markus Stange] First typed array: Allow profile.shared.stackTable.frame to be an Int32Array (#6087) [Nazım Can Altınova] Show more user friendly errors for unsupported profile version in both the frontend and the cli (#6107) [fatadel] Expose counter information in profiler-cli (#6084)
Other Changes:
[Nazım Can Altınova] Bump profiler-cli version to 0.3.0 (#6104) [Mark

entry.title: Autofill Permits Wrong 'Name on Card'
entry.content: Hello, Several e-commerce sites have forms for payment card details, and some of them have a field for ‘Name on Card’. Some sites fill in this field with what they think is the name on the card and, in my experience, it is often wrong. If the user deletes the mistaken value autofill will insert the correct name where it was, but, if the aberrant entry is left in place, autofill will not change it, and the payment can be refused. Would it be an idea to have autofill insert the correct name even i

entry.title: What are your favorite Firefox-based productivity workflows for research and presentations?
entry.content: Hi everyone, I use Firefox for most of my research because of its privacy features and tab management, but I still spend a lot of time turning webpages and articles into presentation slides. Has anyone found a good workflow for this? I recently started looking at MagicSlides AI PPT Maker for converting online content into presentations, and I'm curious if other Firefox users have similar tools or extensions they recommend.

entry.title: what is it Mozilla Connect Community ?
entry.content: what is it Mozilla Connect Community ?

entry.title: Firefox sucks.
entry.content: Firefox is the worst browser I've used. It crashes my device constantly and I will never use it again.

entry.title: Einstellungsmenü
entry.content: Hallo, ich werde von Firefox mit folgender Nachricht angelogen: "Gleiche Einstellungen, neues Erscheinungsbild! Wir haben diese Seite neu gestaltet, damit Sie sie leichter überfliegen und erkunden können. Ihre persönlichen Einstellungen haben sich nicht geändert, und alles ist weiterhin vorhanden. Tipp: Nutzen Sie die Suchfunktion, um direkt zu dem zu gelangen, was Sie suchen." ...und dann auch noch gefragt ob ich das verstanden habe. Das Clownsyndrom ist ein globales Problem. Mit einem neuen un

entry.title: Disable Switch to tab on iOS
entry.content: I only want the search/address bar to make suggestions from bookmarks: It is currently not an option on iOS 152.0 to disable “Switch to tab”. Am I missing something here? It is infuriating because I never use the function and it forces me to do scrolling , wasting time. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Issue with Save-As dialog on MacOS
entry.content: In version 149 I could move the Save As dialog to see information on a webpage I was trying to save. In version 152, the Save As dialog is locked to the page and obscures information I need to see to complete the operation. Please restore the version 149 behavior for the Save As dialog. Thanks. 2019 MacBook Pro, MacOS 26.5.1

entry.title: WTH. My Thunderbird is sending TWO of my emails addresses. ONE is suppose to be for private use. THANKS!
entry.content: All of a sudden, I go to send an email on one account. and Account I use on people I do not trust yet, And ALL OF A SUDDEN, the email is put in my Most protected email account, as sent. And when I LOOK, the sent email has BOTH emails as "FROM". Talk about insecure and a violation. Now my IMPORTANT and PROTECTED email is out there with people I don't trust so much. Thanks. Is it from a recent Botched Update? The work Ahead is gonna kill me .

entry.title: Thunderbird 152 automatically creates a folder named “thunderbird”
entry.content: When starting Thunderbird Linux version 152, it automatically creates a folder named “thunderbird” (without a dot) in the home directory. The bug appeared in version 152, not in version ESR 140. Even after clearing the data, it's still like this. OS: Arch Linux 2026, Desktop: GNOME

entry.title: Independent voices wallpaper
entry.content: Hello! The "Independent Voices" wallpaper collection is currently available on Firefox for Android, but it seems to be missing from the desktop version. Can you guys make these wallpapers available on Firefox for desktop as well? If that's not possible, at least providing a download link for users who want to use them as wallpapers. Thanks =D

entry.title: 浏览器启动之后，会存在上下两个标题栏
entry.content: 浏览器版本：firefox nightly 154.0a1（2026-06-27） 操作系统版本：windows10 家庭中文版 22h2 在第一次打开浏览器的时候，会出现两个标题栏（截图见附件），重新大小化之后重复标题栏消失。浏览器进程存在的情况下，打开第二个浏览器，不会再出现这个情况。但只要将浏览器全部关闭再启动，仍然会出现两个标题栏

entry.title: Widgets keep turning back on
entry.content: I hate the addition of widgets to the home page (or whatever you call the page that appears when you open a new tab). They're distracting and irrelevant. I turn them off... and every few days they're back on again. My question is not so much "why is this happening?", but more "why don't you respect our choices, and leave the things we've disabled, disabled?" Similarly, "Support Firefox" and "sponsored shortcuts" keep getting turned back on without my consent. This behaviour does not leave a favo

entry.title: Gemini side panel edge reacts to hover but not to click — possible drag-handle bug
entry.content: have a Gemini subscription that enables the Gemini assistant inside Gmail. When the Gemini side panel is open and I move my mouse cursor near the panel's edge, the cursor changes to a resize arrow, suggesting the panel is draggable. However, clicking and dragging that edge does nothing — the panel doesn't actually resize. This seems like a broken resize handle: the hover state is triggered correctly, but the click-and-drag interaction doesn't follow through. Could this be a bug in how the panel

entry.title: thinwnphrmsakdi804@gmail.com
entry.content: thinwnphrmsakdi804@gmail.com

entry.title: Privacy setting
entry.content: On my ipad mini 7 ios 26.5 in the upper left hand of the Firefox website, I am still seeing the mask that is supposed to mean I am in privacy mode but I turned that tab setting to OFF in the Firefox setting , so why is that mask still there and how do i get rid of that mask that represents privacy setting when i donot want it .

entry.title: Firefox randomly pegs one CPU core (70–100%) with "Isolated Web Content" even on simple websites
entry.content: I've been trying to get to the bottom of a Firefox performance issue, and after weeks of troubleshooting I'm running out of ideas. The main problem is that Firefox will randomly drive one CPU core to 70–100% usage. When this happens, my laptop temperature quickly rises to around 80–90°C and the fans ramp up. The process responsible is usually "Isolated Web Content." What's confusing is that this doesn't only happen on complex websites. I've seen it while watching YouTube videos, but I've also se

entry.title: System level FFmpeg support for Firefox
entry.content: I am not a Firefox developer but looking at the code there is FFmpeg included at dom/media/platforms/ffmpeg. Unfortunately there is no support for using the system level FFmpeg if available. This would allow for up to date audio/video codec support. Would it be possible the use the system level FFmpeg in a future Firefox version?

entry.title: Video seeking is now significantly faster on macOS, making scrubbing and jumping to a point in a...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Changed / Bug 2049301 Video seeking is now significantly faster on macOS, making scrubbing and jumping to a point in a video much more responsive.

entry.title: Firefox now supports Iterator.prototype.includes(), allowing developers to check whether an...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Developer / Bug 2034104 Firefox now supports Iterator.prototype.includes() , allowing developers to check whether an iterator contains a given value without first converting it to an array.

entry.title: Firefox now supports the sibling-index() and sibling-count() CSS functions. These functions...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2045706 Firefox now supports the sibling-index() and sibling-count() CSS functions. These functions allow an element to be styled with calculations that use its index among its siblings and its total count of siblings (including itself), respectively.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly now supports two new keywords closest-corner and...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2045278 Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly now supports two new keywords closest-corner and farthest-corner for the radial size in the circle() and ellipse() functions.

entry.title: Exceptions for sites that have their cookies & site data cleared on shutdown can now be set...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / New / Bug 1767271 Exceptions for sites that have their cookies & site data cleared on shutdown can now be set without exempting the site from tracking protection and other cookie restrictions.

entry.title: Thunderbird Monthly Development Digest: June 2026
entry.content: Welcome back from the Thunderbird development team! The past few months have been exceptionally busy across the project. As we approach the midpoint of the year, we’ve been focused on a mixture of delivering user-facing features, investing in long-term architectural improvements, and preparing for the next ESR cycle. A significant amount of effort has gone […] The post Thunderbird Monthly Development Digest: June 2026 appeared first on The Thunderbird Blog .

entry.title: MozPhab 2.15.3 Released
entry.content:
Bugs resolved in Moz-Phab 2.15.3:
bug 2049293 [moz-phab] Init errors get swallowed by Jujutsu backend bug 2049296 [moz-phab] Add jj util backend name check for Jujutsu integration
Discuss these changes in #engineering-workflow on Slack or #Conduit Matrix. 1 post - 1 participant Read full topic

entry.title: The many journeys of learning Rust
entry.content: This is another post in our series covering what we learned through the Vision Doc process. We previously described the overall approach and what we learned about doing user research, we explored what people love about Rust, dug into what it takes to ship safety-crticial Rust, and described some of the major challenges that people face when using Rust. In this post we walk through what folks have found on their journey to learn the Rust programming language with ups and downs covered. As a discl

entry.title: Firefox 152.0.3
entry.content: Firefox 152.0.3 release notes.

entry.title: The JSON Viewer now shows a breadcrumb path at the bottom for the selected entry.
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Developer / Bug 1850288 The JSON Viewer now shows a breadcrumb path at the bottom for the selected entry.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 153, Nightly builds support the new JPEG XL image format, which generally...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2040074 Starting with Firefox 153, Nightly builds support the new J PEG XL image format , which generally has better compression than WebP, JPEG, PNG and GIF and is designed to supersede them.

entry.title: This Week in Rust 657
The web is evolving. So are we.
entry.content: Earlier this month, we officially stood up Mozilla.org: a new 501(c)(3) nonprofit created to steward the long term success of the Mozilla Project. Over the last year or so, I’ve said a lot about how AI is reshaping the web — and how we need to simultaneously stand up for the open internet Mozilla helped build […] The post The web is evolving. So are we. appeared first on The Mozilla Blog .

entry.title: Thundermail June 2026 update: what we learned after the first few waves of invites
entry.content: Over the past several weeks, we have been welcoming early users from our waitlist into Thundermail, a few waves at a time. Many of you are now setting up your accounts, trying things out, and sharing your thoughts with us. Naming updates You may have noticed that we are now saying Thundermail more often, and […] The post Thundermail June 2026 update: what we learned after the first few waves of invites appeared first on The Thunderbird Blog .

entry.title: Keeping the Web Open and Private in the Bot Era
entry.content: If you’ve been running into endless CAPTCHAS or website login requests lately, you’re not imagining things. Websites, facing a rising tide of abusive traffic from bots, are adopting increasingly aggressive countermeasures, damaging user’s experience of the web, their privacy and open access to the web. In this post, we’ll talk about a new initiative we’re […] The post Keeping the Web Open and Private in the Bot Era appeared first on The Mozilla Blog .

entry.title: PACT: Anonymous Credentials for the Web
entry.content: This is the technical companion to our update on Distilled, “Keeping the web open and private in the bot era.” Here we take a deeper look at the problem space, the design we’re proposing, and the problems still left to solve. Bots (and privacy-preserving browsers) not welcome Browse a news site in a private window. Shop […] The post PACT: Anonymous Credentials for the Web appeared first on Mozilla Hacks - the Web developer blog .

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the progress() CSS function. This is a...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2047015 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the progress() CSS function. This is a math function that can be used in calculations to compute the position of a value relative to a start and an end.

entry.title: Added a time picker user interface to <input type="time"> and <input...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 1726107 Added a time picker user interface to <input type="time"> and <input type="datetime-local">, making selecting a time more accessible and intuitive.

entry.title: Building Competitive Digital Markets: From Rules to Results
entry.content: Two years ago, the Digital Markets Act was the first of its kind: an ex ante competition framework introducing contestability and supporting innovation. But, as much as its critics try … Read more The post Building Competitive Digital Markets: From Rules to Results appeared first on Open Policy & Advocacy.

entry.title: Firefox for Android 152.0.2
entry.content: Firefox for Android 152.0.2 release notes.

entry.title: Firefox 152.0.2
entry.content: Firefox 152.0.2 release notes.

entry.title: Eyedropper Quick Action, geckodriver 0.37, and Tighter File Permissions – These Weeks in Firefox: Issue 205
entry.content: Highlights Dao added a new Eyedropper quick action! Check it out by typing “color” or “eyedropper” in the URL bar (Bug 1803575) on Nightly. Henrik Skupin released geckodriver 0.37.0, which … Read more

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly ships an experimental implementation of the...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2046153 Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly ships an experimental implementation of the link-parameters property by Tyler Thrailkill.

entry.title: Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly now supports the sibling-index() and sibling-count()...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform Starting with Firefox 153, Firefox Nightly now supports the sibling-index() and sibling-count() CSS functions. These functions allow an element to be styled with calculations that use its index among its siblings and its total count of siblings (including itself), respectively. Documentation .

entry.title: Firefox 152.0.1
entry.content: Firefox 152.0.1 release notes.

entry.title: Share any open page with a QR code. Right-click a tab, select Share > Generate QR Code. Great...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 1996422 Share any open page with a QR code. Right-click a tab, select Share > Generate QR Code. Great for posters, invitations, banners, and other printed materials. On macOS, you can also right-click the address bar to access the Share menu.

entry.title: Quickly pick and copy a color from any page by typing "pick color", "color picker", or...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 1803575 Quickly pick and copy a color from any page by typing "pick color", "color picker", or "eyedropper" in the address bar and selecting the "Pick a color" quick action.

entry.title: Firefox Labs can now be opened quickly by typing "labs" or "experiment" in the address bar and...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2023169 Firefox Labs can now be opened quickly by typing "labs" or "experiment" in the address bar and selecting the Open Firefox Labs quick action.

entry.title: New Deploy of PerfCompare (June 17th)
entry.content:
The latest version of PerfCompare is now live! Check out the change-log below to see the updates: [andra - esanuandra ]
PCF-391 The keyboard handling for the revision dropdown should be done differently (#926)
[david - davidmiculit ]
Bug 2040649 - Remove Raptor framework from PerfCompare (#1042)
[kala - kala-moz ]
Results Page: Return the scatter series for base and new back to original order (#1046)
Bug: 2044194 Port client-side KDE mode detection from kde-widget into CommonGraph (#1045

entry.title: This Week in Rust 656
Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the alpha() CSS function. This is a...
entry.content: 154.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2047437 Starting with Firefox 154, Firefox Nightly now supports the alpha() CSS function. This is a relative color function that can be used to modify just the alpha component of a given color.

entry.title: Firefox WebDriver Newsletter 152
entry.content: Firefox 152 adds Private Browsing support for extension installs, enforces screenshot dimension limits, improves download behavior, and fixes navigation error reporting.

entry.title: Firefox Profiler Deployment (June 16, 2026)
entry.content:
The latest version of the Firefox Profiler is now live! Check out the full changelog below to see what’s changed: Highlights:
[Nazım Can Altınova] Add source map symbolication and source view support (#6018)
It requires Firefox changes that will land in Firefox 154, but after these changes, you will be able to see the source mapped function names as well as the source contents!
[fatadel] Upgrade to React 19 (#6067) [fatadel] Drive counter tooltips from a tooltipRows schema (#6023) [Markus St

entry.title: Firefox is easier than ever to customize
entry.content: Firefox gives you many ways to make the browser your own, from privacy settings and AI controls to tab management, custom colors, and more. As we continue to improve Firefox, you get more control over how it works for you. Today, we’re introducing a redesigned settings experience that makes your options easier to find, understand, […] The post Firefox is easier than ever to customize appeared first on The Mozilla Blog .

entry.title: What’s new in Firefox this June, and what’s next on the Firefox roadmap
entry.content: Firefox has been busy introducing updates across productivity, privacy and AI. From Project Nova and browser-wide AI controls to expanded privacy protections and new ways to stay organized, the goal is simple: help you spend less time managing your browser and more time getting things done online. But building the best browser isn’t just about […] The post What’s new in Firefox this June, and what’s next on the Firefox roadmap appeared first on The Mozilla Blog .

entry.title: Mobile Progress Report: June 2026
entry.content: The past month was busy; the theme was evolution. We went into this quarter with our own ideas for what we wanted to accomplish. However, our users had better ideas. With the release of Thunderbird’s own mail service, Thundermail, the need for a better account settings import process across our services and apps became vital. […] The post Mobile Progress Report: June 2026 appeared first on The Thunderbird Blog .

entry.title: Firefox 140.12.0esr
entry.content: Firefox 140.12.0esr release notes.

entry.title: MFSA 2026-60 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 152
entry.content: MFSA 2026-60 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 152

entry.title: MFSA 2026-61 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 140.12
entry.content: MFSA 2026-61 Security Vulnerabilities fixed in Thunderbird 140.12

entry.title: Firefox 152.0
entry.content: Firefox 152.0 release notes.

entry.title: Firefox 115.37.0esr
entry.content: Firefox 115.37.0esr release notes.

entry.title: Giving You More Control – These Weeks in Firefox: Issue 204
entry.content: Highlights Maxx Crawford added a pref to hide the New Tab logo so users can opt out of branding without altering page layout or resorting to CSS overrides. Harshit enabled … Read more

entry.title: High dynamic range video is now supported in Firefox on Windows 10 and 11 for HDR displays...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2025322 High dynamic range video is now supported in Firefox on Windows 10 and 11 for HDR displays connected to AMD and NVIDIA GPUs (notably this excludes Intel+NVIDIA laptops for now), work is underway to enable this feature on more GPU vendors. To use this feature you must ensure that HDR mode is enabled for the relevant displays in Windows Settings - Display. HDR videos may appear brighter in Firefox than in other browsers, which will be improved in a future release, as we

entry.title: Extensions can no longer access local files by default. Users can grant or revoke this access...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Changed / Bug 2034168 Extensions can no longer access local files by default. Users can grant or revoke this access via the new "Access local files on your computer" permission, separate from "Access your data for all websites".

entry.title: Firefox now highlights the location permission icon in red whenever a website has access to your...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2038194 Firefox now highlights the location permission icon in red whenever a website has access to your location. The permission icon is also now visible on search results pages where it was previously hidden.

entry.title: Firefox now supports a limited subset of the non-standard ::-webkit-scrollbar pseudo-element to...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2038877 Firefox now supports a limited subset of the non-standard ::-webkit-scrollbar pseudo-element to improve web compatibility: Rules with non-zero width / height disable overlay scrollbars for the affected container. display: none on ::-webkit-scrollbar behaves like scrollbar-width: none , hiding the scrollbar. @supports selector(::-webkit-scrollbar) now evaluates to true. Full styling capabilities (colors, border-radius, etc.) are intentionally not supported

entry.title: It is now possible to merge multiple PDFs by dragging a PDF in the PDF sidebar.
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2043290 It is now possible to merge multiple PDFs by dragging a PDF in the PDF sidebar.

entry.title: Added improved support for videos with overlays so users can more easily access video actions...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2041819 Added improved support for videos with overlays so users can more easily access video actions from context menus.

entry.title: The Intl.LocaleInfo proposal adds the ability to query Locale information, such as week data...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2037069 The Intl.LocaleInfo proposal adds the ability to query Locale information , such as week data (first day in a week, weekend start day, weekend end day), text direction and hour cycle used in the Locale.

entry.title: It is now possible to add images as new pages within PDFs using the Firefox PDF editor.
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2032967 It is now possible to add images as new pages within PDFs using the Firefox PDF editor.

entry.title: Firefox now verifies and displays Qualified Website Authentication Certificates (QWACs) in...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 2043399 Firefox now verifies and displays Qualified Website Authentication Certificates (QWACs) in accordance with eIDAS regulations.

entry.title: The Picture In Picture API exposes a way for web developers to make use of user agent...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 1463402 The Picture In Picture API exposes a way for web developers to make use of user agent Picture-In-Picture functionality.

entry.title: Developers can now use the text import attribute to import text files using the module system.
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2039881 Developers can now use the text import attribute to import text files using the module system.

entry.title: The newly added Error.stackTraceLimit property allows to set the maximal depth of the captured...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2037856 The newly added Error.stackTraceLimit property allows to set the maximal depth of the captured JavaScript error stack as a performance optimization.

entry.title: Text selected in PDFs is now highlighted clearly like in normal web pages. The selection color...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Changed / Bug 1879559 Text selected in PDFs is now highlighted clearly like in normal web pages. The selection color also follows OS settings like high-contrast mode.

entry.title: The circle() and ellipse() functions accept two new keywords closest-corner and farthest-corner...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2037673 The circle() and ellipse() functions accept two new keywords closest-corner and farthest-corner for their radial size.

entry.title: Implemented the new popover=hint spec, which removes a series of inconsistent and unexpected behaviors.
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2029974 Implemented the new popover=hint spec, which removes a series of inconsistent and unexpected behaviors.

entry.title: The vertical-align property was converted into a shorthand for baseline-source,...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2010717 The vertical-align property was converted into a shorthand for baseline-source , alignment-baseline , and baseline-shift in Firefox 149. Now, alignment-baseline supports the new keywords alphabetic , ideographic , central , mathematical , and hanging , extending the variety of baselines to choose for alignment .

entry.title: Updated HTML parsing rules for <select> elements, aligning with web standards to support...
entry.content: 153.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2019977 Updated HTML parsing rules for <select> elements, aligning with web standards to support future customizable select dropdown features.

entry.title: Added support for Apple's system-wide full-screen keyboard command (Globe-F)
entry.content: 153.0a1 / New / Bug 1748950 Added support for Apple's system-wide full-screen keyboard command (Globe-F)

entry.title: Site zooming via keyboard or mouse now supports more and smaller increments.
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Changed / Bug 2036853 Site zooming via keyboard or mouse now supports more and smaller increments.

entry.title: The Pointer Lock API now supports the unadjustedMovement option, allowing sites to receive raw...
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Web Platform / Bug 2037802 The Pointer Lock API now supports the unadjustedMovement option, allowing sites to receive raw mouse-movement data unaffected by OS-level acceleration.

entry.title: When a website in a Private Browsing window may be broken because of tracker blocking, Firefox...
entry.content: 152.0a1 / New / Bug 2035600 When a website in a Private Browsing window may be broken because of tracker blocking, Firefox now shows an infobar after a reload. Clicking "Reload" in the infobar disables tracker blocking for that tab and site and reloads the page. All other tracking protections remain active.

entry.title: Improved the reliability of saving images when dragging images to the desktop or Finder windows on macOS.
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Fixed / Bug 643096 Improved the reliability of saving images when dragging images to the desktop or Finder windows on macOS.

entry.title: GTK: Fixed the direction of word-based selection commands in RTL text fields.
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Fixed / Bug 1138419 GTK: Fixed the direction of word-based selection commands in RTL text fields.

entry.title: Fixed a number of text editing commands involving arrow keys in right-to-left languages on macOS.
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Fixed / Bug 1425483 Fixed a number of text editing commands involving arrow keys in right-to-left languages on macOS.

entry.title: In multiple monitor situations, the About Firefox window now more reliably opens on the display...
entry.content: 152.0a1 / Fixed / Bug 1868738 In multiple monitor situations, the About Firefox window now more reliably opens on the display with the most recently-used Firefox window.
